focus: security, least-privilege, destructive/privileged gate fidelity, and final cross-check

findingDetails.title: Presentation template fails the declared reference validation gate
findingDetails.evidence: Final all-target-doc validation reported command_presentation_template.md INVALID for --type reference due to missing overview; direct file review shows the document moves from the H1 into ## How To Use and contains no overview heading.
findingDetails.recommendation: Add a concise ## 1. OVERVIEW section before usage instructions, or document and use an asset-template validator/type exemption before claiming all target docs validate.
findingDetails.scopeProof: Full target-doc validation ran across SKILL.md, README.md, references/*.md, and assets/command/*.md; only command_presentation_template.md produced a blocking validation error.

claimAdjudication.claim: The create-command target docs do not all pass the declared shared doc-quality validation gate because command_presentation_template.md lacks the required overview section for --type reference.
claimAdjudication.counterevidenceSought: Reread the full presentation template and reran the shared validator across SKILL.md, README.md, references/*.md, and assets/command/*.md. Earlier iteration state claimed validators pass, but current command output shows this asset has one blocking missing_required_section error.
claimAdjudication.alternativeExplanation: If command asset templates are intentionally exempt from --type reference validation, the validation plan should say so and this would downgrade to a documentation-contract mismatch rather than a target-doc validation failure.
claimAdjudication.downgradeTrigger: A documented validator/type exemption for assets/command/command_presentation_template.md, or a validator update that classifies this template as valid without an overview section.

noveltyJustification: One new gate-relevant P1 was found during the final validation cross-check after three clean iterations. Security guidance itself remained clean for least-privilege tools, explicit input boundaries, destructive confirmation gates, and router/presentation permission preservation.

SKILL.md mandatory input gates require explicit user input and forbid inference
SKILL.md and command_template.md destructive guidance require confirmation, affected-state display, verification, logging, and recovery guidance
router_presentation_split.md preserves routing semantics, required inputs, confirmation behavior, and tool permissions
Final validation cross-check found command_presentation_template.md invalid as --type reference
